body.summary: Ace Alliance finds the proposed “Dingo: a Production-Grade Block Producer in Go by Blink Labs” Treasury Withdrawal Governance Action constitutional. Rationales are archived at https://github.com/ace-alliance/ace-voting/
body.rationaleStatement:
A [PDF version][pdf-link] of this rationale is also made available.

[pdf-link]: https://ipfs.io/ipfs/QmXvMsayfoxncwTVSovefUk5hHMufNUpWd56d58Wj5v3fF

This Treasury Withdrawal Governance Action (TWGA) requests 6,900,000 ada to fund twelve months of engineering on the Dingo Cardano node.

Under Article II.6, governance actions must use a standardized format, include an immutable URL and hash, and provide sufficient rationale. This TWGA satisfies these requirements by utilizing an immutable IPFS URL and hash, and providing a title, abstract, justification, and supporting materials.

Under Article II.7, TWGAs must meet six specific requirements. This TWGA satisfies all of them:

* **II.7.1 (Terms):** Specifies the 12-month period, costs, purpose, and refund circumstances via a smart contract "Failsafe Sweep".  
* **II.7.2 (Disclosure):** Discloses prior Catalyst funding and confirms no recent treasury withdrawals were received.  
* **II.7.3 (Net Change Limit):** A Net Change Limit must be set and not exceeded. The 350M ADA NCL is treated as set (see Precedent), and this ₳6,900,000 request does not exceed the ₳339,858,000 remaining balance.  
* **II.7.4 (Audits):** Allocates $500,000 for periodic independent security audits.  
* **II.7.5 (Administrators):** Designates an Independent Oversight Board as administrators.  
* **II.7.6 (Custody):** Enforces auto-abstain delegation and prohibits SPO delegation for all escrowed funds held by the administrator smart contract prior to disbursement.
body.precedentDiscussion:
Under Article II.7.3, a Net Change Limit (NCL) must be set for a TWGA to be found Constitutional. Accordingly, we look to the “Net Change Limit (Epoch 613 to Epoch 713)” (gov\_action1m3xx08yv788vfxqh6nfvrjtvmqpwezsy0ggaczctkyjmttc2wmxsq4jsr7q) governance action (“350M ADA NCL”).

In our voting rationale for the “Amaru Treasury Withdrawal 2026,” we addressed the fact that the 350M ADA NCL Info Action received only 57% CC approval due to three non-votes, falling short of the required 67% threshold. We applied a narrow exception: because the non-votes reflected a mistaken procedural view rather than substantive opposition to the NCL itself, we would defer to the CC's aggregate vote on the Amaru TWGA to determine the constitutional validity of the 350M ADA NCL.

Because the Amaru TWGA successfully met the DRep and CC approval threshold and was enacted on March 25, 2026Ace Alliance considers the 350M ADA NCL to be definitively set and active.
body.counterargumentDiscussion:
A valid counterargument is that the Constitution does not authorize displacing the on-chain voting results of an Info Action to reconsider the outcomes of subsequent TWGAs. As we noted in our Amaru rationale, this argument is persuasive and, under ordinary circumstances, would be the prevailing view.

However, we previously determined that we must balance this against Article I.1.9, which mandates that all users of the Cardano Blockchain shall be treated fairly and impartially. It would be unjust to penalize TWGA submitters and the Cardano Community for the inaction of three Constitutional Committee members on the 350M ADA NCL action. Having already applied this narrow exception to treat the 350M ADA NCL as validly set based upon successful passage of the Amaru TWGA, we consistently apply that established precedent here. The NCL is active, and this proposal operates validly within its remaining balance.
body.conclusion: Ace Alliance finds the proposed “Dingo: a Production-Grade Block Producer in Go by Blink Labs” Governance Action constitutional.

references.label: Ace Alliance Voting Rationale for Amaru Treasury Withdrawal 2026
